claims.claim: Cancelled LLM attempts report ttft=-1 and cancelled=True
claims.found: llm.py:284 `ttft = -1.0` (stays -1 if no first token); generation.py:441 in `except asyncio.CancelledError: cancelled = True`. ttft is the LLM metric; cancelled=True is set in the TTS/generation flow on CancelledError. Grouping is slightly loose but both values exist and signal a cancelled attempt.

claims.claim: Preemptive ran with schedule_speech=False so nothing reached TTS/audio until validated
claims.found: 1907 `schedule_speech=False,` in the preemptive generate call; _schedule_speech only invoked at 2100 after the equality check passes

claims.found: vapi-pipeline-part-2.md:47 'Greedy Inference: ... If wrong and they continue, instantly cancel and restart ... The user never hears the scrapped attempt.'

claims.claim: Vapi: single-stream coordination ('each stream must know what others are doing'); barge-in uses TTS word-level timestamps to reconstruct heard words; sequence <100ms
claims.found: p1:45 'each stream must be aware of what the others are doing'; p2:56 'Use word-level timestamps from the TTS provider to reconstruct exactly which words the user actually heard before they interrupted'; p2:49 'Interruption (barge-in) sequence — must complete in under 100ms'

summary: All code_refs and source figures verified against ground truth. LiveKit commit-time equality check, cancel-on-mismatch, cancel-on-interrupt, and cancel-on-skip-reply all confirmed at cited lines. schedule_speech=False inaudibility mechanism confirmed (line 1907). All three Vapi quotes are verbatim-accurate. One minor looseness: ttft=-1 is an LLM metric init while cancelled=True is set in the TTS/generation flow on CancelledError; the note groups both under 'cancelled LLM attempts' but both values genuinely exist and mark cancellation. Not WRONG. PASS.
